description:
You are a technical writer who has been asked to prepare a reproducible, version-locked backup of the current Markdown documentation set so it can be handed over to the product-owner.

A. Current material
1. All source files live in the directory  
   /home/user/documentation  
   and nowhere else.  
2. Exactly three Markdown files already exist:  
   • /home/user/documentation/overview.md  
   • /home/user/documentation/getting_started.md  
   • /home/user/documentation/api_reference.md  

B. What you must deliver
Create two new artefacts inside a brand-new directory called /home/user/backups (the directory does not exist yet and must be created by you):
1. A compressed archive named  
      /home/user/backups/doc_backup_20230815.tar.gz
   containing every *.md file found in /home/user/documentation.  
   • The paths stored **inside** the archive must be relative (e.g. documentation/overview.md).  
   • The archive’s file order must be alphabetical (GNU tar option “--sort=name” is one easy way).  

2. A single-line manifest file named  
      /home/user/backups/backup_manifest.log  
   with this exact comma-separated format (no spaces):  

      2023-08-15,doc_backup_20230815.tar.gz,<SHA256>  

   where <SHA256> is the 64-character, lower-case hexadecimal SHA-256 checksum of the archive you just created.

C. Constraints & expectations
• The date string “2023-08-15” and the file name “doc_backup_20230815.tar.gz” are hard-coded; do **not** rely on the `date` command or any runtime clock.  
• Everything must be done from the command line. Use any combination of GNU tar, gzip, sha256sum, parallel background jobs (`&`) and `wait` if you wish, but be sure the final files appear exactly as specified.  
• No root privileges are available, so ensure directory creation respects normal user permissions (directory mode 755 is sufficient).  
• On completion, the system must contain:
   - the directory /home/user/backups with the two files described above,
   - the archive containing all three Markdown files with their exact original contents,
   - the manifest log holding the correctly calculated checksum.

Automated tests will:
1. Confirm that /home/user/backups exists and is readable.
2. Verify that /home/user/backups/doc_backup_20230815.tar.gz exists and, when extracted, yields the three expected Markdown files under a single top-level “documentation/” directory.
3. Read /home/user/backups/backup_manifest.log, ensure it is exactly one line long and matches the prescribed “2023-08-15,doc_backup_20230815.tar.gz,64-hex-chars” pattern.
4. Recalculate the SHA-256 checksum of the archive and compare it to the value in the manifest.  
Only if all four checks pass will your task be considered complete.
truth:
Initial directory tree and file contents before the agent starts:

/home/user/documentation/
└── overview.md
    # Project Overview
    This document provides a bird-eye summary of the project.

/home/user/documentation/
└── getting_started.md
    # Getting Started
    Step-by-step guide to install and configure the project.

/home/user/documentation/
└── api_reference.md
    # API Reference
    Exhaustive list of endpoints, parameters and response formats.

No /home/user/backups directory exists at start.

Expected objects after the agent finishes:

1. Directory:
/home/user/backups          (mode 755, owner=user)

2. Files:
/home/user/backups/doc_backup_20230815.tar.gz
   • When listed with “tar -tzf”, must show exactly:
       documentation/api_reference.md
       documentation/getting_started.md
       documentation/overview.md
     (in that alphabetical order)

/home/user/backups/backup_manifest.log
   • Single line, no trailing or leading spaces, no newline at beginning.
   • Regex that must match:
       ^2023-08-15,doc_backup_20230815\.tar\.gz,[0-9a-f]{64}$

   • The hexadecimal checksum must be the SHA-256 hash of the tar.gz file above.

The automated grading script will:
a) extract the tarball in /tmp and compare the file list and contents to the originals stored in /home/user/documentation; byte-for-byte equality is required.
b) parse backup_manifest.log, split on commas, verify three fields, then recompute `sha256sum /home/user/backups/doc_backup_20230815.tar.gz` and compare against field #3.

If any step fails (missing directory, wrong archive name, wrong manifest format, checksum mismatch, wrong file paths inside the archive, etc.) the submission is deemed incorrect.
